title: Inline validation: email required when submitting empty form
description: Verifies the email field shows an inline required validation message and the user remains on the sign up page.

title: Inline validation: invalid email format
description: Verifies an invalid email format is rejected with an inline error and submission does not proceed.

steps.description: Type "ValidPass123!" into the confirm password field

title: Inline validation: password strength requirements not met
description: Verifies weak passwords are blocked and a visible password strength hint/error is shown.

steps.description: Type "weak" into the confirm password field

title: Inline validation: confirm password must match password
description: Verifies mismatched confirm password shows an inline error and prevents account creation.

steps.description: Type "ValidPass123?" into the confirm password field

title: Submission loading state is visible while creating account
description: Verifies a visible loading/submitting state appears after clicking Create Account.

steps.description: Type "ValidPass123!" into the confirm password field

title: Sign out successfully from Dashboard redirects to Sign in
description: Verifies that a signed-in user can sign out from the dashboard navbar dropdown and is redirected to the sign-in page.

steps.description: Click the user avatar in the navbar
Click the "Sign out" button in the dropdown

title: Sign out dropdown closes and returns user to Sign in
description: Verifies the sign-out action completes and the user ends up on the sign-in screen (not left on an authenticated view).

steps.description: Click the user avatar in the navbar
Click the "Sign out" button in the dropdown

title: User dropdown reveals Sign out option on Dashboard
description: Verifies that the navbar user dropdown can be opened and contains a visible Sign out option before clicking it.

steps.description: Click the user avatar in the navbar

title: Already authenticated user visiting /auth/signin is redirected to /dashboard
description: Verifies that authenticated users cannot use the sign-in page and are redirected to the dashboard.

title: Already authenticated user visiting /auth/signup is redirected to /dashboard
description: Verifies that authenticated users cannot use the sign-up page and are redirected to the dashboard.

title: Landing page loads and core marketing sections are visible
description: Verifies the landing page renders key sections (hero, feature grid, preview mockup, steps) for first-time visitors.

steps.description: Verify element "app preview mockup" is visible
Verify element "three-step flow diagram" is visible
Verify element "code output section" is visible

title: Get started CTA routes to Sign up
description: Verifies the primary CTA on the landing page navigates to the signup page.

title: Header Sign in link routes to Sign in
description: Verifies the header Sign in link on the landing page navigates to the signin page.

title: Landing page content remains accessible after scrolling
description: Verifies long-form landing page sections are reachable and visible when scrolling.

steps.description: Verify element "code output section" is visible

title: Landing page CTAs remain visible and actionable after scrolling
description: Verifies users can still find and use primary navigation elements after scrolling through sections.

title: Open dashboard and view project cards list
description: Verifies the dashboard loads successfully and displays the project cards list (or an explicit empty state) after data fetch.

steps.description: Verify element "project cards list or empty state" is visible

title: Navigate from a project card to project detail page
description: Verifies clicking a project card from the dashboard navigates to the project detail view.

steps.description: Click on the first visible project card
Verify element "project detail page content" is visible

title: Filter projects by name using dashboard search and clear search
description: Verifies the search input filters the visible project cards and clearing the input restores the full list.

steps.description: Verify element "filtered project cards" is visible
Verify element "project cards list" is visible

title: Search with no matching results shows no-results state
description: Verifies entering a search query that matches nothing shows a clear no-results state.

steps.description: Verify element "no results state" is visible
Verify element "project cards list" is not visible

title: Delete a project via card delete button and confirm deletion success
description: Verifies confirming deletion removes the project card and shows a success toast.

steps.description: Click the delete button on the first visible project card

title: Cancel project deletion in AlertDialog keeps project card
description: Verifies canceling the delete confirmation closes the dialog and leaves the project card in place.

steps.description: Click the delete button on the first visible project card
Click the "Cancel" button in the AlertDialog

title: Start creating a new project from dashboard
description: Verifies the New Project button from the dashboard navigates to the create project route.

steps.description: Verify element "create new project" is visible

title: Dashboard shows loading skeleton while projects are loading
description: Verifies a loading skeleton is shown during initial dashboard load before the project list is rendered.

steps.description: Verify element "project cards list or empty state" is visible

title: Prevent submission when file is missing (with project name filled)
description: Verifies the create action is blocked or validation is shown when no file is selected even if the project name is provided.

steps.description: Click the "New Project" button or link
Type "No File Selected Project" into the project name field

title: Create project successfully by pasting valid OpenAPI JSON
description: Verifies a user can paste valid OpenAPI JSON, see character count, create the project, and be redirected to the project detail page.

steps.description: Type "Paste JSON Success" into the project name field

title: Show invalid JSON syntax alert, then fix and create successfully
description: Verifies invalid JSON is rejected with an alert, and fixing the JSON allows project creation and redirect.

steps.description: Type "Invalid JSON Then Fix" into the project name field
Verify text "Invalid JSON syntax" is visible

title: Prevent create when paste content is empty
description: Verifies the user is blocked from creating a project when no content is provided and sees the expected alert.

steps.description: Type "Empty Paste Content" into the project name field
Verify text "No content provided. Please upload a file, paste content, or fetch from a URL." is visible

title: Character count increases after pasting content
description: Verifies the character count indicator updates after content is pasted into the textarea.

title: Show invalid YAML syntax error when pasting malformed YAML
description: Verifies malformed YAML pasted into the textarea triggers an invalid YAML syntax error on create.

steps.description: Type "Bad YAML" into the project name field

Type "openapi: 3.0.0
info:
  title: BadYaml
  version 1.0.0
paths: {}" into the paste textarea
Verify text "Invalid YAML syntax" is visible

title: Reject non-OpenAPI JSON content
description: Verifies JSON that is syntactically valid but not an OpenAPI document is rejected with a non-OpenAPI error.

steps.description: Type "Not OpenAPI JSON" into the project name field

title: Create project successfully by pasting valid OpenAPI YAML
description: Verifies a user can paste valid OpenAPI YAML and successfully create a project and be redirected to the project detail page.

steps.description: Type "Paste YAML Success" into the project name field

Type "openapi: 3.0.0
info:
  title: YAML Pets
  version: 1.0.0
paths:
  /pets:
    get:
      responses:
        '200':
          description: OK
" into the paste textarea

title: Create project successfully by fetching a valid OpenAPI spec URL
description: Verifies a user can fetch a spec from a valid URL, see a success message and preview, create the project, and land on the project detail page.

steps.description: Type "URL Fetch Project" into the project name field
Type "https://example.com/openapi.json" into the URL input field
Verify text "Spec fetched successfully" is visible

title: Invalid URL format shows validation error and allows correction
description: Verifies invalid URL format is rejected with an error message and the user can correct the URL input afterward.

steps.description: Type "Bad URL Format" into the project name field
Type "not-a-url" into the URL input field
Verify text "Invalid URL format" is visible
Type "https://example.com/openapi.json" into the URL input field

title: Remote 404 fetch shows failure message and user can retry with another URL
description: Verifies a valid-looking URL that returns 404 shows a fetch failure error and the user can retry after changing the URL.

steps.description: Type "404 Fetch Project" into the project name field
Type "https://example.com/does-not-exist.json" into the URL input field
Verify text "Failed to fetch: 404" is visible
Type "https://example.com/openapi.json" into the URL input field

title: Loading state appears while fetching spec from URL
description: Verifies the UI shows a visible loading state during the fetch operation.

steps.description: Type "Loading State Project" into the project name field
Type "https://example.com/openapi.json" into the URL input field

title: Create Project is blocked until a spec is successfully fetched and previewed
description: Verifies the project cannot be created before a successful spec fetch, and becomes creatable after success.

steps.description: Type "Blocked Create Project" into the project name field
Type "https://example.com/openapi.json" into the URL input field
Verify text "Spec fetched successfully" is visible

title: Switching away and back to Fetch from URL preserves entered project name
description: Verifies the project name remains present when the user changes tabs within the new project page and returns to the URL fetch tab.

steps.description: Type "Name Persistence" into the project name field

title: Create button prevented/handled when no spec input is provided
description: Verifies the UI shows a clear error and does not create a project when the user clicks Create Project with no spec provided.

steps.description: Click the "New Project" button or "Create Project" CTA on the dashboard
Ensure the spec text area field is empty

title: Project detail page renders core layout for an owned project
description: Verifies the project detail page shows the header, two-column layout, and default Endpoints tab for an existing owned project.

steps.description: Click on the first project card in the projects grid

title: Project detail page shows two-column layout with endpoints table and server config panel
description: Verifies the project detail page visually contains an endpoints table on the left and a server configuration panel on the right.

title: Project header shows editable project name control
description: Verifies the project name appears in the header as an editable field/control.

Verify element "Project name input" is visible

title: Navigate back to dashboard from project detail using back arrow
description: Verifies the back arrow in the project header returns the user to the dashboard.

Click the back arrow in the project header

title: Switching to Preview tab shows preview content area
description: Verifies the Preview tab is present and displays a visible preview/code area when selected.

title: Switching to History tab shows history content area
description: Verifies the History tab is present and displays a visible history section when selected.

steps.description: Click on the first project card in the projects grid

title: Project detail shows endpoint table columns and summary bar counts
description: Verifies the endpoint explorer table renders expected endpoint details and the summary bar is visible on the project detail page.

Verify element "Summary" bar with total endpoints and type counts is visible

title: Endpoint table shows method badge and path values for at least one row
description: Verifies at least one endpoint row displays a method badge and a path value in the table.

Verify element "Method" badge in the first endpoint row is visible
Verify element "Path" value in the first endpoint row is visible
Verify element "MCP Type" dropdown in the first endpoint row is visible

title: Endpoint table shows operation ID, summary, and params count for at least one row
description: Verifies the endpoint row exposes operation ID, summary text, and params count fields in the table.

steps.description: Click on the first project card in the dashboard
Verify element "Operation ID" value in the first endpoint row is visible
Verify element "Summary" value in the first endpoint row is visible
Verify element "Params" count in the first endpoint row is visible

title: Loading skeleton appears while endpoint mappings load
description: Verifies a loading skeleton is shown during initial data load on the project detail page.

steps.description: Click on the first project card in the dashboard
Verify element "Loading" skeleton for the endpoint table is visible
Verify element "MCP Type" dropdown is visible

title: Empty state appears when filters yield no endpoints
description: Verifies the endpoint table shows the empty state message when filters result in no matches.

steps.description: Click on the first project card in the dashboard
Type "zzzz-no-match" into the table search or filter input
Verify text "No endpoints match filters" is visible

title: Search by path substring filters endpoint table and clearing restores results
description: Verifies typing into the endpoint search filters rows by path substring and clearing the search restores the full list.

steps.description: Click the first project card in the projects grid
Type "/" into the endpoint search input

title: Search by operationId filters endpoint table and clearing restores results
description: Verifies the search input filters endpoints by operationId text and clearing returns to the unfiltered table.

steps.description: Click the first project card in the projects grid
Type "get" into the endpoint search input

title: Combined filters apply AND logic across method and MCP type
description: Verifies selecting an HTTP method and an MCP type applies combined (AND) filtering to the endpoints table.

steps.description: Click the first project card in the projects grid
Select "GET" from the HTTP method filter dropdown
Select "Tool" from the MCP type filter dropdown
Verify text "No endpoints match filters" is not visible

title: Clear method filter by selecting All Methods keeps other filters applied
description: Verifies clearing the method filter via All Methods removes the method constraint while leaving other filters (e.g., MCP type) in effect.

steps.description: Click the first project card in the projects grid
Select "POST" from the HTTP method filter dropdown
Select "Resource" from the MCP type filter dropdown
Select "All Methods" from the HTTP method filter dropdown

title: Clear MCP type filter by selecting All Types keeps method filter applied
description: Verifies clearing the MCP type filter via All Types removes the MCP constraint while leaving the HTTP method filter in effect.

steps.description: Click the first project card in the projects grid
Select "PUT" from the HTTP method filter dropdown
Select "Tool" from the MCP type filter dropdown
Select "All Types" from the MCP type filter dropdown

title: No-results state appears when search matches no endpoints
description: Verifies an empty result set shows the 'No endpoints match filters' message when search text matches nothing.

steps.description: Click the first project card in the projects grid
Type "zzzz-no-such-endpoint" into the endpoint search input
Verify text "No endpoints match filters" is visible

title: Pagination resets to page 1 when applying search filter
description: Verifies changing the search query resets pagination back to the first page of endpoint results.

Type "get" into the endpoint search input

title: Pagination resets to page 1 when changing method filter
description: Verifies selecting an HTTP method filter resets pagination back to the first page.

steps.description: Click the first project card in the projects grid
Verify element "Previous" pagination button is visible
Select "DELETE" from the HTTP method filter dropdown

title: Pagination controls appear and default count text is correct when project has >20 endpoints

Verify the pagination controls (Previous and Next buttons) are visible

title: Navigate to next page updates range text and page indicator
description: Verifies clicking Next advances the endpoint table to the next page and updates the visible page status.

steps.description: Click on a project card that is known to have more than 20 endpoints

title: Navigate back to previous page restores first page range
description: Verifies clicking Previous after moving forward returns to the prior page and restores the earlier range text.

steps.description: Click on a project card that is known to have more than 20 endpoints

title: Projects with 20 or fewer endpoints do not show pagination controls
description: Verifies endpoint pagination UI is hidden when the endpoint count does not exceed one page.

steps.description: Click on a project card that is known to have 20 or fewer endpoints
Verify text "Showing 1-20 of" is not visible
Verify the pagination controls (Previous and Next buttons) are not visible

title: Total endpoint count remains visible across pagination navigation
description: Verifies the UI continues to display the total endpoint count while moving between pages.

steps.description: Click on a project card that is known to have more than 20 endpoints
Verify text "of" is visible within the "Showing" summary
Verify text "of" is visible within the "Showing" summary

title: Change MCP type to Tool and save successfully (optimistic update + counts refresh)

steps.description: Click on the first project card in the dashboard grid
Verify element "Endpoint mappings table" is visible
Click MCP type dropdown on an endpoint row
Verify text "Tool" is visible in the same endpoint row

title: Successful save shows loading indicator during persistence
description: Verifies a loading/saving indicator appears after selecting a new MCP type and disappears after save completes.

steps.description: Click on the first project card in the dashboard grid
Verify element "Endpoint mappings table" is visible
Click MCP type dropdown on an endpoint row

title: Summary counts refresh after save
description: Verifies that changing one endpoint’s type causes the on-page summary counts to refresh after the save finishes.

steps.description: Click on the first project card in the dashboard grid
Click MCP type dropdown on an endpoint row

title: Change MCP type to Exclude and verify table reflects it immediately
description: Verifies selecting Exclude is applied optimistically in the endpoint row.

Verify element "Endpoint mappings table" is visible
Click MCP type dropdown on an endpoint row
Verify text "Exclude" is visible in the same endpoint row

title: Changing MCP type does not navigate away from the project detail page
description: Verifies the dropdown interaction and save keep the user on the same project detail screen.

steps.description: Click on the first project card in the dashboard grid
Verify element "Endpoint mappings table" is visible
Click MCP type dropdown on an endpoint row

title: Repeated change on the same row updates to the latest selected type
description: Verifies the endpoint row reflects the most recent selection when switching types multiple times.

steps.description: Click on the first project card in the dashboard grid
Verify element "Endpoint mappings table" is visible
Click MCP type dropdown on an endpoint row
Click MCP type dropdown on the same endpoint row
Verify text "Tool" is visible in the same endpoint row

title: Bulk set selected endpoints to Resource shows success toast and clears selection
description: Verifies selecting multiple endpoints enables the bulk bar, bulk update succeeds with correct count message, and the bulk bar disappears afterward.

steps.description: Click on the first project card in the dashboard grid
Verify element "endpoint mappings table" is visible
Check the checkbox for the first endpoint row
Check the checkbox for the second endpoint row
Verify element "bulk action bar" is visible
Click "Set as Resource" in the bulk action bar
Verify element "bulk action bar" is not visible

title: Select all visible rows then bulk set to Tool shows success toast and clears selection
description: Verifies the header select-all checkbox selects visible rows, bulk action updates them, and the bulk bar disappears after success.

steps.description: Click on the first project card in the dashboard grid
Verify element "endpoint mappings table" is visible
Check the select all checkbox in the endpoint table header
Verify element "bulk action bar" is visible
Click "Set as Tool" in the bulk action bar
Verify element "bulk action bar" is not visible

title: Bulk set to Template works for a multi-selection and shows updated count toast
description: Verifies the Template bulk action is available and produces a success toast indicating the number of updated endpoints.

Check the checkbox for the first endpoint row
Check the checkbox for the third endpoint row
Verify element "bulk action bar" is visible
Click "Set as Template" in the bulk action bar
Verify element "bulk action bar" is not visible

Verify element "endpoint mappings table" is visible
Check the checkbox for the second endpoint row
Verify element "bulk action bar" is visible
Click "Exclude" in the bulk action bar
Verify element "bulk action bar" is not visible

title: Bulk action bar does not appear when no endpoints are selected
description: Verifies the bulk action bar remains hidden until at least one endpoint row is selected.

Verify element "endpoint mappings table" is visible
Verify element "bulk action bar" is not visible
Verify element "Set as Tool" is not visible

title: Deselecting all endpoints hides the bulk action bar
description: Verifies that removing the final selection causes the bulk action bar to disappear.

Verify element "endpoint mappings table" is visible
Check the checkbox for the first endpoint row
Verify element "bulk action bar" is visible
Click the checkbox for the first endpoint row to deselect it
Verify element "bulk action bar" is not visible

title: Success toast count matches number of manually selected rows (2)
description: Verifies the success toast reflects the number of endpoints selected when performing a bulk action.

Check the checkbox for the first endpoint row
Check the checkbox for the second endpoint row
Verify element "bulk action bar" is visible

title: Selecting auth method auto-saves

title: Preview tab shows default index.ts with syntax highlighting and line numbers
